title: Can You Really Quit the Internet
subtitle: 戒网四年半：普通人的数字断连有多奢侈

paragraphs.en: Could you get by without using the internet for four and a half years? That's exactly what singer and actress Selena Gomez has done in a bid to improve her mental health. She has spoken extensively about the relationship between her social media usage and her mental wellbeing, recalling feeling like "an addict" when she became Instagram's most followed user in 2016. "Taking a break from social media was the best decision that I've ever made for my mental health," says she. "The unnecessary hate and comparisons went away once I put my phone down."
paragraphs.zh: 你能四年半不使用互联网吗？这正是歌手兼演员赛琳娜·戈麦斯为改善心理健康所做的努力。她曾广泛谈论社交媒体使用与心理健康之间的关系，回忆起2016年成为Instagram上粉丝最多的用户时，自己感觉像个“瘾君子”。“远离社交媒体是我为自己的心理健康做出的最佳决定，”她说，“一旦我放下手机，那些不必要的仇恨和攀比就消失了。”

paragraphs.en: Ditching the web at large, however, is a far more subtle and complicated prospect. The increasing digitisation of our society means that everything from paying a gas bill to plotting a route to a friend's house and even making a phone call is at the mercy of your internet connection. Actively opting out of using the internet becomes a matter of privilege. Ms Gomez's multi-millionaire status has allowed her to take the "social" out of social media, so she can continue to leverage her enormous fame while keeping the trolls at bay.
paragraphs.zh: 然而，完全放弃网络则是一个远为微妙且复杂的前景。我们社会日益加深的数字化意味着，从缴纳燃气费、规划去朋友家的路线，甚至拨打电话，一切都取决于你的网络连接。主动选择不使用互联网已成为一种特权。戈麦斯女士的千万富翁身份让她能够将社交媒体中的“社交”部分剥离出去，这样她就能继续利用自己的巨大名气，同时将网络喷子拒之门外。

paragraphs.en: The fact that she's still the second most-followed woman on Instagram suggests it's entirely possible to maintain a significant web profile to promote various projects — by way of a dedicated team — without being exposed to the cruel comments, hate mail and rape or death threats. It goes without saying that this is fundamentally different from how the rest of us without beauty deals and films to publicise use the likes of Instagram, TikTok and Twitter, but even the concept of a digital detox requires having a device and connectivity to choose to disconnect from.
paragraphs.zh: 她至今仍是Instagram上粉丝数第二高的女性，这一事实表明，完全可以通过一个专门的团队来维持重要的网络形象以推广各类项目，而无需亲自面对残酷的评论、仇恨邮件以及强奸或死亡威胁。不用说，这与我们这些没有美容代言和电影要宣传的普通人对Instagram、TikTok和Twitter的使用方式有着本质区别，但即便是“数字戒断”这个概念本身，也需要先拥有一台设备和网络连接，才能选择断开。

paragraphs.en: The UK's digital divide has worsened over the past two years, leaving poorer families without broadband connections in their homes. Digital exclusion is a major threat to wider societal equality in the UK, so witnessing companies like Facebook championing the metaverse as the next great frontier when school children are struggling to complete their homework feels particularly irritating.
paragraphs.zh: 过去两年，英国的数字鸿沟进一步加剧，许多贫困家庭家中没有宽带连接。数字排斥对英国更广泛的社会平等构成了重大威胁，因此，当看到Facebook等公司把元宇宙吹捧为下一个伟大前沿，而学龄儿童却在为完成作业而苦苦挣扎时，尤其令人恼火。

paragraphs.en: Consequently, it's worth bearing in mind that while deleting all social media accounts will undoubtedly make some feel infinitely better, many other people benefit from the strong sense of community that sharing platforms can breed. Internet access will continue to grow in importance as we edge further towards web 3.0, and greater resources and initiatives are needed to provide the underprivileged with the connectivity they desperately need to learn, work and live.
paragraphs.zh: 因此，值得记住的是，虽然删除所有社交媒体账号无疑会让一些人感觉好得多，但还有许多人受益于分享平台所能孕育的强烈社群归属感。随着我们进一步迈向Web 3.0，互联网接入的重要性将持续增长，需要投入更多资源和举措，为弱势群体提供他们学习、工作和生活所急需的网络连接。

paragraphs.en: It's crucial that people who feel that social media is having a detrimental effect on their mental health are allowed to switch off — and for those living in digital exclusion to be able to switch on in the first place.
paragraphs.zh: 至关重要的一点是，要让那些觉得社交媒体对心理健康产生负面影响的人能够选择关闭它——同时，也要让那些生活在数字排斥中的人首先能够拥有开启它的能力。
